title: Guard write_standup_marker so a marker-write failure cannot fail the SessionStart hook after the briefing prints
evidence: `write_standup_marker` does bare `os.makedirs(...)` + `open(path, "w")` with no try/except, and main() calls it unguarded at line 872. Every other write in this hook (clear_dev_marker, _write_dev_session_owner, heal_statusline_wiring) is wrapped with a never-crash-startup guard; this one is the exception. A read-only/locked `.codearbiter/.markers` dir (or ACL issue) raises OSError, main() exits nonzero, and the linchpin SessionStart hook is reported as failed — and the marker never being writable means the full briefing re-emits every session.
impact: An environment-level FS failure turns the whole SessionStart injection into a hook error; inconsistent with the file's own 'never brick session startup' contract.
recommendation: Wrap the write_standup_marker call (or its body) in the same swallow-and-continue guard used by the sibling marker writers.
acceptance_criteria: An unwritable .markers directory no longer causes session-start.py to exit nonzero
